# Austin Residential Building Codes

## Applicable Codes

The City of Austin enforces the following codes for new residential construction, last updated October 2024:

- **2021 International Residential Code (IRC)** with local amendments
- **2021 International Energy Conservation Code (IECC)** — Austin requires compliance with the more stringent Austin Energy Green Building program for homes over 2,000 sq ft
- **NEC 2023** for electrical installations
- **2021 International Plumbing Code** with Texas amendments
- **2021 International Mechanical Code** for HVAC

## Foundation Requirements

Austin sits on Edwards Limestone and expansive Blackland Prairie clay. The geotechnical report (AG-2025-1192) for 4218 Barton Creek Drive identified a Plasticity Index (PI) of 42, classifying the soil as highly expansive. Requirements:

- Post-tensioned slab foundation required for PI above 35
- Minimum slab thickness: 4 inches with #4 rebar at 18-inch centers both ways
- Moisture barrier: 10-mil polyethylene vapor retarder under slab
- Foundation engineer of record: Dr. Robert Kim, PE (Lone Star Geotechnical)

## Wind Load

Travis County falls under ASCE 7-22 wind speed map at 115 mph ultimate design wind speed, Exposure Category B (suburban). Roof connections require Simpson H2.5A hurricane ties at every rafter-to-wall connection. The standing seam metal roof must be rated for 115 mph wind uplift.

## Energy Code

Austin Energy Green Building requires a minimum 2-star rating (equivalent to IECC 2021 plus local amendments):

- Wall insulation: minimum R-20 cavity or R-13 + R-5 continuous
- Ceiling insulation: R-38 minimum
- Fenestration U-factor: 0.30 max
- HVAC efficiency: 15 SEER2 minimum for cooling, 80% AFUE for gas heating
- Duct leakage: maximum 4 CFM25 per 100 sq ft of conditioned floor area
- Blower door test required: maximum 5 ACH50
